title: rbac — compliance evidence
description: Access control is the load-bearing surface for every framework's access-provisioning + access-review requirement. Every role/permission mutation is audited. System role modifications are refused + logged critical.

controls.principle: logical access controls restrict access to information assets
controls.evidence: PermissionResolver is the SINGLE entry point for every permission check across the codebase. RBAC ∪ grants − denies + delegation composition per design.md §2 D-A3. Deny-priority ensures explicit blocks always win.

Every role assignment fires RoleAssignedToPrincipal + writes an audit row with actor_id + timestamp + reason (see roles.retention). System role modifications are refused + logged at critical severity. Time-bounded assignments (expires_at) support least-privilege review.
GuardMismatch (422) refuses any write that would bind a sanctum permission to a platform_admin role. ApplicationMismatch (422) refuses cross-Application role rows. Both are compliance-audited.
TenantDefaultRoleSeeder on TenantProvisioned. Cascade-revocation on User disable / suspend via HasSessionRevocation trait chain.
RoleAssignmentService enforces the four-eyes principle via #[AsApprovableAction] wiring on assign/revoke actions when the tenant has approvals configured for those actions.
Platform-admin roles (super_admin, security, ops) are isolated by guard boundary. Every platform-plane mutation writes an audit-log entry with actor + reason.
PurgeExpiredRoleAssignmentsJob enforces expiration. `rbac:list-principal-roles --include-expired` supports access-review audits.

controls.principle: removal or adjustment of access rights
controls.evidence: Role revocation cascades through PermissionCacheManager — the compact permission array on Sanctum PATs is updated within seconds. Immediate lockdown available via auth::sessions.revoke_all (breach response).
Scope-suffix convention (.own / .tenant / .branch / .team / .child / .any) narrows permission scope at query-build time. Base repository applies the WHERE clause — principals see only rows in scope.
Default deny at step 5 of the resolver. New permissions without a scope suffix fail the valid_permission_name rule at write time.
Cache invalidation window bounded to <1 second across nodes (Redis pubsub broadcast). Permission changes propagate before the next request lands.

hipaa.reason: Academorix does not process protected health information (PHI) at this tier. Sports academies that record injury notes trigger different controls under `modules/products/sports/medical/`.
